ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific Nonya zhang entry; closest match is the glutinous rice dumpling class. Nonya-specific filling and preparation are not captured.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based composite dish — glutinous rice dumpling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Nonya zhang code; A0CKP (rice-based composite dish) is the closest defensible anchor. Peranakan-specific preparation not captured.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Nonya zhang or glutinous rice dumpling as a distinct clinical food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical Nonya zhang weighs approximately 180 g and constitutes a single serving as a snack or light meal during the Dragon Boat Festival season.

nutrient_source.title: Nonya Zhang (Rice Dumpling) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Nonya zhang (rice dumpling), cr_id F07030007, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central glutinous rice and pork belly component data where HPB does not report these fields.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — glutinous rice
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nonya zhang as a composite dish. GI of 98 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice (sticky rice), which is the dominant carbohydrate component (~85% of total carbohydrate mass). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (98) × carbohydrate per serving (57.1 g from 31.7 g/100g × 180 g serving) / 100 = 55.9. This is a high-GL food.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g with minimal potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: At 300 mg sodium per 100 g and an estimated 540 mg per standard 180 g dumpling, Nonya zhang contributes approximately 27% of the 2000 mg daily sodium limit in a single serving; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to half a dumpling and avoid adding soy sauce or chilli sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glutinous rice GI ~98 with low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very high glycaemic index (~98) of glutinous rice combined with low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100 g) and a glycaemic load of approximately 56 per serving makes Nonya zhang unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, limit to half a dumpling and pair with non-starchy vegetables to partially blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork belly and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pork belly filling contributes saturated fat (estimated 1.7 g/100 g) and the dish delivers approximately 540 mg sodium per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Nonya zhang to occasional festive consumption and choose smaller portions to manage cumulative saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid from pork belly and high-GI refined carbohydrate
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pork belly is a source of arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; combined with glutinous rice as a high-GI refined carbohydrate that promotes systemic inflammation, this dish is best avoided or consumed only occasionally in very small portions by individuals with active RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nonya zhang (娘惹粽) is a Peranakan-style glutinous rice dumpling traditionally prepared and consumed during the Dragon Boat Festival (端午节) in Singapore and Malaysia. Unlike Cantonese-style bak zhang, Nonya zhang is characteristically blue — the glutinous rice is tinted with blue pea flower (butterfly pea flower) juice — and is filled with a sweet-savoury mixture of minced pork, pork belly, dried shrimp, candied winter melon, and aromatic spices including five-spice powder and coriander. The filling is wrapped tightly in pandan or bamboo leaves and steamed until the rice is fully cooked and fragrant. The texture is dense, sticky, and slightly chewy from the glutinous rice, with a rich, umami-forward filling that balances savoury pork with subtle sweetness from the candied melon. Per 100 g, Nonya zhang provides 191 kcal, 5.1 g protein, 4.8 g fat, 31.7 g carbohydrate, 1.1 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The dominant carbohydrate is glutinous rice, which has a very high glycaemic index (~98), making this a high-glycaemic-load food (GL ~56 per 180 g dumpling). Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension due to its high GI and borderline sodium content. The pork belly filling contributes saturated fat and arachidonic acid, raising concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Nonya zhang is a festive, culturally significant dish and is not intended for daily consumption.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Nonya rice dumpling?
Is Nonya zhang suitable for diabetics?
What is the difference between Nonya zhang and bak zhang?
What is the GI of glutinous rice dumpling?
How much sodium is in a Nonya zhang?
Can I eat Nonya zhang if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F07030007) with a source confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for glutinous rice and pork belly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for glutinous rice, which is the dominant carbohydrate source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Nonya zhang as a composite Peranakan dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F07030007) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated_fat_g (1.7 g) and sugar_g (1.5 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice (USDA FDC ID 168878) and pork belly, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions; these are marked as estimated values. GI of 98 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for glutinous rice (sticky rice) as the dominant carbohydrate component. Nonya zhang is not halal due to pork content. The dish is a seasonal festive item associated with the Dragon Boat Festival and is not intended for regular daily consumption.
